message:
feat(tools): harden reservations, remove broadcast messaging, improve search and product UIDs

Broadcast messaging is now fully blocked with an explicit error message. The
broadcast=true code path, including the entire agent enumeration and bulk-send
loop, has been removed. This prevents agents from spamming each other and
enforces the intentional design that recipients must be addressed individually.

Reservation system improvements:
- relativize_path now properly normalizes paths using component-based traversal
  instead of string-based depth counting, correctly collapsing parent-dir
  references and handling backslash-to-slash conversion
- ReservationIndex uses patterns_overlap for exact-entry conflict checks,
  catching directory prefix overlaps (e.g. "src" vs "src/main.rs") that
  string equality missed
- Glob conflict checks now also examine root-level exact entries when the
  request has a scoped prefix
- Empty pattern ("") is now rejected as too-broad alongside "*" and "**"
- Released reservations are skipped in build slot conflict detection
- TTL values are capped at 1 year (31,536,000 seconds) to prevent unbounded leases
- Reservation renewal now archives via WBQ (write-behind queue) for durability
- file_reservation_paths response includes created_ts for audit trail

Search and product improvements:
- search_messages and search_messages_product now accept a cursor parameter for
  stable pagination across large result sets
- Product UID generation widened from 20 to 32 hex chars with zero-padded
  formatting for better uniqueness guarantees
- fetch_inbox_product sort tiebreaker changed from ascending to descending ID
  for consistent newest-first ordering
- summarize_thread_product adds ID-based tiebreaker to timestamp sort
- Thread ID sanitization now strips leading non-alphanumeric chars before the
  emptiness check
- Markdown task-list extraction supports *, +, and case-insensitive [x] markers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
